frozen_components: LLM (used for hypothesis proposal and reasoning)
architecture.summary: HYVIN is a framework that grounds LLMs to environments through iterative hypothesis-verification-induction cycles. The LLM proposes hypotheses of sub-goals; these are verified by interaction with the environment (BabyAI simulator); successful verifications yield generalized skills that are learned and reused for more complex downstream tasks. Information flows from LLM reasoning → environment interaction → skill extraction → skill injection back into LLM planning.

biblio.title: Hypothesis, Verification, and Induction: Grounding Large Language Models with Self-Driven Skill Learning
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: HYVIN achieves comparable performance in the most challenging tasks compared with imitation learning methods that cost millions of demonstrations
HYVIN autonomously grounds the LLM onto the environment without requiring fine-tuning or pre-defined behavior APIs for every single task
Learned skills can be utilized to accomplish more complex tasks that fail to pass the verification phase
